video_title: When Life Gives You Tangerines | Official Trailer | Netflix
reply_text: I couldn't agree more with you that unconditional love is what really matters in life. The story of three generations of women in this drama emphasizes a mother's unwavering love for her child. In order to prevent Ae-sun from becoming a sea maiden, her mother, Gwang-rye, worked till the end of her life. She wanted Ae-sun to be allowed to follow her ambition of becoming a poet. Despite becoming a housewife, Ae-sun sacrificed everything for her daughter Geum-myeong to be able to attend college and study overseas. In addition to that, Gwan-sik has given his all to the family as a husband and a father. As a husband, he supports his wife unconditionally and gives her love and care. As a father, he supported his family by doing dangerous fishing work and sold his house to give his daughter a better education. Although he worked hard throughout his life and only lived to be 56, his death was tragic for many people since they believed he should have had a comfortable and happy old age. We all sleep long, but having loved ones worth dying for is something that makes life worth living.

video_title: When Life Gives You Tangerines | Official Trailer | Netflix
reply_text:
Finally watched all the episodes of When Life Gives You Tangerines yesterday, and I was completely swept away from the start. Every moment hit me right in the heart. I laughed through the warmth, cried through the pain, and felt so deeply connected to every character’s journey. I do hope for a second season, but at the same time, there’s something perfect about the way it ended. It was raw, tender, and hauntingly beautiful… easily one of the most unforgettable Korean dramas I’ve ever experienced.

As a Korean American, the story resonated with me on an even deeper level. My parents went through a similar path, and watching this felt like revisiting pieces of their past. It already feels like a nostalgic memory I’ll carry with me for a long time. A++

video_title: When Life Gives You Tangerines | Official Trailer | Netflix
reply_text: The most touching part of this drama was seeing Gwan Sik’s injured finger that he couldn’t even fold. It reminded me so much of my dad in Korea—someone who dedicated his entire life to working hard for me and our family. That moment hit me the hardest.
